title: All Guts No Glory

jokes.text: Carlton, relax. This is the line for class registration... not the Nixon Library.
jokes.setup: Carlton is acting overly anxious about college registration
jokes.punchline: Will compares his anxiety to something as historically serious as the Nixon Library
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor that plays on Carlton's uptight nature and Will's laid-back perspective. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Yes, I'll have what she's having.
jokes.setup: Academic advisor asks if they can help Will with course selection
jokes.punchline: Will treats course registration like ordering at a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with excellent economy. References the famous 'When Harry Met Sally' line while fitting Will's character.

jokes.text: We were on the freeway.
jokes.setup: Will says they talked to every woman they saw
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals this happened while driving on the freeway
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that takes Will's flirtatious behavior to an absurd extreme. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: The same reason Eddie Murphy tries to sing, I like the challenge.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks why Will does crazy things
jokes.punchline: Will compares himself to Eddie Murphy's singing attempts
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that works as both self-aware humor and character insight. Good structure.

jokes.text: Carlton, the challenge is to make your life as easy as possible.
jokes.setup: Carlton questions Will's easy course choices
jokes.punchline: Will reframes laziness as a philosophical challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will logic - turning his laziness into a virtue through twisted reasoning. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: That's why God made canned meat. Spam cakes, anyone?
jokes.setup: Will's philosophy about making life easy
jokes.punchline: Offers Spam cakes as the ultimate easy food
jokes.explanation: Extends the 'easy life' philosophy to absurd food choices. Fits Will's street-smart but lazy character.

jokes.text: Patchouli, sweet of you to notice.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil compliments something smelling good, expecting food
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals it's his hippie-scented cologne, not cooking
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent character work. Geoffrey's dry delivery and the unexpected hippie reference work beautifully.

jokes.text: Western Pornography.
jokes.setup: Will is challenged to name his philosophy class
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Philosophy' as 'Pornography'
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism that gets a big laugh but relies on predictable mispronunciation humor. Still effective for the character.

jokes.text: Did anybody really believe Kool Moe Dee was a cowboy?
jokes.setup: Will claims to understand Western Philosophy
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates his misunderstanding with a hip-hop/western reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific pop culture reference that perfectly captures Will's street-smart but academically confused perspective. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The wild wild west The wild wild west / I used to live downtown
jokes.setup: Will's Kool Moe Dee reference
jokes.punchline: Will actually performs the rap, complete with dancing
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that showcases Will Smith's charisma and fits the character perfectly. Visual gag inferred from context.

jokes.text: Hilary, this is your sister, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley is asked to show sisterly compassion to heartbroken Hilary
jokes.punchline: Ashley introduces herself to her own sister
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan setup for the real punchline. Ashley's formal introduction to her own sister is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Suck it up, and get on with your life!
jokes.setup: Ashley formally introduces herself to offer comfort
jokes.punchline: Delivers brutal tough love instead of comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. Ashley's blunt 'comfort' is hilariously harsh and well-structured.

jokes.text: And you don't have to. Here. I'll even look the number up for you. Hello, Neiman's?
jokes.setup: Phil suggests Hilary could go shopping to feel better, she says she's not ready to go out
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately calls the store for her and Hilary starts ordering
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing both Phil's enabling and Hilary's instant recovery when shopping is involved. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: Hilary immediately calling Neiman's and shopping by phone
jokes.setup: Hilary claims she's not ready to go out shopping
jokes.punchline: She immediately starts shopping by phone with catalog numbers memorized
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Hilary's shopping addiction transcends her heartbreak

jokes.text: Okay, okay, attention Kmart shoppers. We're running out of books for Psych 157... the study of human sexuality. So for any of you slimmies who miss out... I'll be more than happy to demonstrate the contents of the book. Twice, you know.
jokes.setup: Bookstore announcement about psychology textbooks
jokes.punchline: Worker offers to 'demonstrate' human sexuality textbook content
jokes.explanation: Somewhat predictable sexual humor but fits the college bookstore setting and character's sleazy personality.

jokes.text: You're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Bookstore worker's inappropriate offer
jokes.punchline: Blunt, immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective response. Perfect economy and timing as a reaction beat.

jokes.text: Hey, Sting, kick some lyrics, man.
jokes.setup: Will approaches a blonde student
jokes.punchline: Mistakes her for Sting based on hair color
jokes.explanation: Classic Will mistake based on superficial observation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: No, you do that all by yourself. What'd you sign up for today, Advanced Sarcasm?
jokes.setup: Will asks if Carlton always embarrasses him
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic comeback about Advanced Sarcasm class
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that shows Carlton can hold his own against Will's teasing

jokes.text: Have you ever heard of a little thing called Western Philosophy?
jokes.setup: Will brags about taking a difficult class
jokes.punchline: Names the class he's been consistently mispronouncing and misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier jokes about Will's academic confusion. Ironic confidence about a subject he doesn't understand.

jokes.text: Is that Spam in your teeth?
jokes.setup: Will tries to impress with his 'intellectual prowess'
jokes.punchline: Student notices remnants of his earlier Spam consumption
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that undermines Will's attempt to look sophisticated. Perfect deflation of his pretensions.

jokes.text: I didn't have no Spam.
jokes.setup: Caught with Spam in his teeth
jokes.punchline: Double negative denial that actually confirms it
jokes.explanation: Classic Will grammar mistake that makes his denial ineffective. Good character consistency with his speech patterns.

jokes.text: Well, Hair Club For Men would be a good start.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks for help politely
jokes.punchline: Worker insults Carlton's hair/appearance
jokes.explanation: Generic appearance-based insult. Gets a laugh but lacks originality or character depth.

jokes.text: Nothing like that minimum-wage humor.
jokes.setup: Bookstore worker's insult
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismisses it as 'minimum-wage humor'
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that's both witty and perfectly in character for privileged Carlton. Great economy and class-conscious humor.

jokes.text: You know, I'd stay and continue this battle of wits with you... but you're obviously unarmed.
jokes.setup: Extended verbal sparring with bookstore worker
jokes.punchline: Classic 'battle of wits/unarmed' insult
jokes.explanation: Classic insult that's well-delivered but completely unoriginal. Works for Carlton's pretentious character but lacks creativity.

jokes.text: He dribbles behind his back... he's up and he shoots. Oh, and it's no good. Carlton the peacock says, 'Don't be a quitter, pick up your litter.'
jokes.setup: Worker crumples Carlton's form
jokes.punchline: Narrates throwing it away like a basketball shot, mocking Carlton's mascot role
jokes.explanation: Creative visual gag with sports commentary. The mascot reference adds character-specific humiliation.

jokes.text: Help me! Come on, guys, help me up. Yeah, we need a cleanup on aisle four. We got some bird droppings.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably falls or gets knocked down
jokes.punchline: Worker continues the bird/mascot mockery with 'cleanup' announcement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation that extends the bird theme. Good follow-through on the established dynamic.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, that's right, it was in the dictionary next to:
jokes.setup: Will says he's seen the girl's picture somewhere before
jokes.punchline: Sets up 'dictionary next to' format for an insult
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for a dictionary insult. Predictable structure but the cut-off leaves audience to fill in the punchline.

jokes.text: Nope, all clear. And... Plus, I don't have any early classes, that means we could sleep in.
jokes.setup: Girl asks if Will has anything better to do than hit on her
jokes.punchline: Will checks his schedule and immediately suggests sleeping together
jokes.explanation: Classic Will smoothness that's both confident and inappropriate. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Just think of me as the Love Terminator.
jokes.setup: Girl says Will is relentless
jokes.punchline: Will compares himself to the Terminator movie
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Will's confidence. Sets up the next joke perfectly.

jokes.text: T2, meet my boyfriend, T-Rex.
jokes.setup: Will calls himself the Love Terminator (T2 reference)
jokes.punchline: Girl introduces her massive boyfriend as T-Rex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that tops Will's Terminator reference. Perfect economy and sets up the physical threat beautifully.

jokes.text: Hasta la vista, baby.
jokes.setup: Facing the intimidating boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Will uses Terminator quote while presumably running away
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that completes the Terminator thread while showing Will's retreat. Good physical comedy moment.

jokes.text: Well, not with those looks.
jokes.setup: Will asks if he can get by
jokes.punchline: Another appearance-based insult
jokes.explanation: Repetitive humor that's getting stale. The worker's one-note character is wearing thin.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, but I don't speak geek.
jokes.setup: Carlton uses sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses it as 'geek' language
jokes.explanation: Standard Will vs. Carlton intellectual clash. Predictable but fits their dynamic well.

jokes.text: Plan A was derailed by this refrigerator-shaped gentleman over here.
jokes.setup: Will explains his class clown plans
jokes.punchline: Describes the intimidating boyfriend as 'refrigerator-shaped'
jokes.explanation: Good callback with creative description. 'Refrigerator-shaped' is both vivid and funny.

jokes.text: I'm Will Smith. And this is an interesting coincidence.
jokes.setup: Professor introduces himself as Jeremy Mansfield
jokes.punchline: Will introduces himself with his real name, calling it a coincidence
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke where Will Smith the actor's name becomes part of the show's humor. Unique fourth-wall moment.

jokes.text: Excuse me, Professor Mansfield, it's not too late to start sucking up, is it?
jokes.setup: Professor describes his difficult course
jokes.punchline: Will immediately asks about sucking up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will response - immediately looking for the easy way out. Good character consistency and timing.

jokes.text: Thank you, Padre.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions the 'widow-maker' final exam
jokes.punchline: Will treats him like a priest giving last rites
jokes.explanation: Good death/religion reference in response to 'widow-maker.' Excellent economy and fits Will's street-smart humor.

jokes.text: No, no, no! No, wait. Call my mother! Somebody call the governor! I ain't even supposed to be in this class, man! I wanna live.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates electric shock equipment
jokes.punchline: Will panics like he's facing execution
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and physical comedy. Will's over-the-top panic is perfectly in character and builds on the death penalty theme.

jokes.text: It's the only course you're taking that actually requires staying awake.
jokes.setup: Registrar guesses Will wants to drop Western Philosophy
jokes.punchline: Implies all Will's other courses are so easy he can sleep through them
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that calls back to Will's academic laziness. Good burn with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Yeah, and I'm sure my wife has a really good reason for cheating on me.
jokes.setup: Will says he has a good reason for dropping
jokes.punchline: Registrar compares it to his wife's infidelity
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark humor that establishes this character's bitter worldview. Good setup for his extended rant.

jokes.text: My wife has seen more action than Chuck Norris.
jokes.setup: Registrar's marital problems
jokes.punchline: Compares wife's affairs to action movie star
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the affair theme with clever wordplay on 'action.' Continues building this character's desperation.

jokes.text: I got a toilet in my closet! And every day I just see my career going down it!
jokes.setup: Registrar describes his terrible office situation
jokes.punchline: Visual of toilet plus metaphor for his failing career
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag combined with perfect metaphor. The escalation of this character's misery is well-crafted and memorable.

jokes.text: Make it one of your demands... when you climb the bell tower with a rifle.
jokes.setup: Will asks for another way to drop the class
jokes.punchline: References campus shooter scenario
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that caps the registrar's breakdown. Edgy for the era but fits the character's complete desperation.

jokes.text: It's celery, I swear.
jokes.setup: Phil is caught eating something while celebrating his court victory
jokes.punchline: Defensive claim about what he's eating (presumably not celery)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil moment - caught indulging and making unconvincing health claims. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: The incarceration of a felon always gets my juices pumping.
jokes.setup: Phil's excitement about his court victory
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about legal success
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's passion for justice with sexual undertones. Fits his personality well.

jokes.text: You should have seen me deny that man's plea bargain. I was brilliant!
jokes.setup: Phil's arousal from legal victory
jokes.punchline: Gets more excited describing his courtroom performance
jokes.explanation: Continues Phil's unusual arousal from legal work. Good escalation that builds the running gag.

jokes.text: Rain check? Vivian, I am not a car wash.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggests a rain check on intimacy
jokes.punchline: Phil compares himself to a car wash
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that's both funny and perfectly captures Phil's frustration. Original and well-structured.

jokes.text: I'm ready for my wax job.
jokes.setup: Car wash metaphor established
jokes.punchline: Phil continues the metaphor with sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the car wash metaphor logically. Maintains the running gag effectively.

jokes.text: I don't want anybody in here who doesn't wanna be here.
jokes.setup: Will expects the professor to refuse his drop request
jokes.punchline: Professor seems to easily agree
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that sets up the real trap. Subverts Will's (and audience's) expectations effectively.

jokes.text: Psych. Or should I say 'philosp.'
jokes.setup: Professor seems to let Will drop easily
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was a trick (psych) with philosophy wordplay
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal with clever wordplay combining 'psych' and 'philosophy.' Well-structured trap for Will.

jokes.text: Do you wanna know the truth? You can't handle the truth!
jokes.setup: Professor asks about wanting to know the truth
jokes.punchline: Will does Jack Nicholson impression from 'A Few Good Men'
jokes.explanation: Famous movie quote that's recognizable and gets a laugh but lacks originality. Fits Will's pop culture knowledge.

jokes.text: Captain Kirk, now that's fly. No, this is The Fly: 'Help me! Help me!'
jokes.setup: Professor tells Will to 'beam out' (Star Trek reference)
jokes.punchline: Will plays with 'fly' meanings, references horror movie
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting multiple pop culture references. Shows Will's quick wit and cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: They all been on Montel Williams.
jokes.setup: Professor asks what different groups have in common
jokes.punchline: Will suggests they've all appeared on talk shows
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about 90s talk show culture. Perfect Will answer that's both wrong and insightful about media.

jokes.text: See, I can't hear you because you no longer exist.
jokes.setup: Will gives a wrong answer
jokes.punchline: Professor pretends Will has ceased to exist
jokes.explanation: Creative professor response that fits the philosophy theme. Absurdist humor that works within the academic setting.

jokes.text: But you could shove me, though, right?
jokes.setup: Professor claims Will doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: Will tests the logic with physical reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical response that challenges the professor's absurd claim. Shows Will's street-smart intelligence.

jokes.text: Man! I should have never dropped out of his class.
jokes.setup: Will watches the engaging philosophy class
jokes.punchline: Realizes he made a mistake dropping the interesting class
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's growth and recognition of his mistake. Sets up his desire to return.

jokes.text: Is that a yes or a no?
jokes.setup: Phil says he's already eaten
jokes.punchline: Will knows Phil will eat anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Phil's appetite. Great economy and shows Will knows his uncle well.

jokes.text: To what? A pie-eating contest? No, to having a fool as a nephew.
jokes.setup: Will asks what challenge Professor Langford gave Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil turns it into an insult about Will
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff with perfect character work. Phil's quick wit and Will's setup create a great burn.

jokes.text: But enough about me, Uncle Phil. Let's talk about you.
jokes.setup: Phil delivers serious criticism of Will's behavior
jokes.punchline: Will deflects with classic avoidance technique
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection that perfectly demonstrates Phil's point about his avoidance of responsibility.

jokes.text: What's the matter, Will? Can't move those oranges at the off-ramp?
jokes.setup: Will claims emergency
jokes.punchline: Professor assumes he's selling fruit on the street
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing professor's assumptions about Will while being witty

jokes.text: Well, yesterday's reality isn't necessarily today's.
jokes.setup: Professor asks what happened to yesterday's Will
jokes.punchline: Will quotes the professor's own philosophy lesson
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Will actually learned something. Good character growth moment with humor.

jokes.text: You come to me, my son, asking for a favor. I'm gonna make you an offer you can't refuse.
jokes.setup: Will asks to get back into class
jokes.punchline: Professor does Godfather impression
jokes.explanation: Predictable movie reference but fits the power dynamic. Gets a laugh but lacks originality.

jokes.text: You come to me, my son, asking for a favor. I'm gonna make you an offer you can't refuse.
jokes.setup: Will asks for favor to get back in class
jokes.punchline: Professor does Godfather impression
jokes.explanation: Standard movie reference but works to show professor has sense of humor

jokes.text: You gotta love the irony, though, huh?
jokes.setup: Professor explains Will must take extra credit without getting credit
jokes.punchline: Professor appreciates the philosophical irony
jokes.explanation: Good philosophical humor that fits the professor's character. The irony is genuinely funny and well-observed.

jokes.text: I mean, is it anywhere near a subway or anything like that?
jokes.setup: Will's confused response to philosophy question
jokes.punchline: Asks about subway proximity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's urban perspective applied to philosophical questions. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay, all right, how about if it landed on, like, a gopher or something like that? Hey, does the noise that the gopher makes count?
jokes.setup: Will's continued attempts to solve the philosophy problem
jokes.punchline: Introduces gopher victim and questions about its noise
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that adds absurdist elements while staying in character. Shows Will's creative but confused thinking.

jokes.text: Or what if it falls on Uncle Phil and he hurt the tree?
jokes.setup: Will's philosophical speculation about falling trees
jokes.punchline: References Uncle Phil's weight in a tree-falling scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and escalation. Perfectly combines philosophy with family dynamics and Will's humor style. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Carlton, thank you very much, but I already have my academic... stuff in order.
jokes.setup: Carlton offers course suggestions again
jokes.punchline: Will repeats his earlier confident claim but changes 'priorities' to 'stuff'
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend callback showing Will's growth. The change from 'priorities' to 'stuff' suggests both humility and genuine learning.

tmdb_overview: Registration day finds Will signing up for no-brainer classes, plus one that might truly challenge him - a philosophy course he takes to meet a girl.
